title: Resend a Tote Task Command After a Tote Error or Failed Execution
summary: Use the Resend Tote Task function in the tote management API page to resend a command to a tote that encountered an error or failed to execute, then review tote task status to confirm the resend outcome.
when_to_use: Use when a tote command encountered an error or failed to execute and support needs to resend the command using the Resend Tote Task function shown on the tote management API page.
not_for: Do not use for AGV command recovery; the source distinguishes tote management functions from AGV recovery functions.
Do not use when deeper fault isolation, retry limits, or alternate recovery logic are required; the source does not provide those details.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Identify the affected tote, access the tote management API page, use Resend Tote Task, and review tote task status afterward.

safety_notes: This source presents the action as a support recovery function for tote command errors or failed execution.
Stop and escalate if the resend does not clear the failed condition or the tote continues to show an error after the resend.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to tote management interface or API-backed Resend Tote Task function
Ability to review tote task status

access_required: Access to the tote management API page or equivalent tote management interface
system_preconditions: A tote command has encountered an error or failed to execute
The affected tote can be identified for selection or entry

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the tote management API features, including Get Tote Task and Resend Tote Task.

steps.title: Identify the affected tote
steps.purpose: Determine which tote requires recovery before using the resend function.
steps.instruction: Identify the tote whose command encountered an error or failed to execute.
steps.expected_result: A specific affected tote is identified for the resend action.
steps.healthy_condition: The affected tote is clearly identified and can be entered or selected in the tote management interface.
steps.failure_condition: The affected tote cannot be confidently identified from the available status information.

actions.label: Identify failed tote
actions.exact_action: Identify the tote whose command encountered an error or failed to execute.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tote management API features on the slide, especially Get Tote Task as the status lookup function for tote tasks.

stop_or_escalate_if: The affected tote cannot be identified.
Available tote status information is insufficient to determine which tote failed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Resend Tote Task is used for a tote that encountered an error or failed to execute.
Get Tote Task shows current tote status and all tote tasks.
OCR: Resend Tote Task — Resends a command to a tote that encountered an error or failed to execute.

steps.title: Open the Resend Tote Task function
steps.purpose: Navigate to the tote recovery function used to resend the failed tote command.
steps.instruction: Open the tote management API page and locate the Resend Tote Task function.
steps.expected_result: The Resend Tote Task function is visible and ready for tote selection or entry.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote management API page shows Resend Tote Task among the available tote management features.
steps.failure_condition: The Resend Tote Task function is not available or cannot be located.

actions.label: Open resend function
actions.exact_action: Open the Resend Tote Task function.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Aveva API page for tote management showing Resend Tote Task in the list of available functions.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote management API page is inaccessible.
The Resend Tote Task function is not present in the available interface.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Visible functions include Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.
Training video segment 0069 shows an Aveva API page for tote management with Resend Tote Task among the visible features.

steps.title: Enter or select the affected tote
steps.purpose: Target the resend action to the correct tote.
steps.instruction: Enter or select the affected tote in the Resend Tote Task function.
steps.expected_result: The resend request is prepared for the intended tote.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct tote is selected or entered in the resend function.
steps.failure_condition: The tote cannot be entered, selected, or confirmed as the intended target.

actions.label: Select tote
actions.exact_action: Enter or select the affected tote.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API page context where Resend Tote Task is available for tote-specific actions.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct tote cannot be selected or entered.
There is uncertainty that the selected tote is the one that failed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Resend Tote Task resends a command to a tote that encountered an error or failed to execute.

steps.title: Submit the resend action
steps.purpose: Resend the command to the affected tote.
steps.instruction: Submit the resend action to resend the command to that tote.
steps.expected_result: The system issues a resent command for the selected tote.
steps.healthy_condition: The resend action is accepted and the tote command is resent.
steps.failure_condition: The resend action is not accepted or does not clear the failed condition.

actions.label: Resend tote task
actions.exact_action: Submit the resend action to resend the command to that tote.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Resend Tote Task option on the tote management API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: The resend action does not clear the failed condition.
The tote continues to show an error or failed execution after the resend.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Resend Tote Task — Resends a command to a tote that encountered an error or failed to execute.

steps.title: Verify tote task status after resend
steps.purpose: Confirm whether the resend succeeded and whether tote state updated.
steps.instruction: Check the tote task status afterward to verify whether the command was successfully resent and the tote state updated.
steps.expected_result: Tote task status reflects the resent command outcome and updated tote state if the resend succeeded.
steps.healthy_condition: Get Tote Task or equivalent status view shows the tote task progressing or updated without the prior failed condition.
steps.failure_condition: The tote still shows an error, failed execution, or no meaningful status update after the resend.

actions.label: Review tote task status
actions.exact_action: Check the tote task status afterward to verify whether the command was successfully resent and the tote state updated.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Get Tote Task function on the tote management API page for current tote status and task review.

stop_or_escalate_if: The resend action does not clear the failed condition.
The tote continues to show an error or failed execution after the resend.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Get Tote Task shows the current status of the totes and all their tasks.
OCR: Retrieves the current tote task.

success_criteria: The affected tote is identified and targeted with Resend Tote Task.
The resend action is submitted for the selected tote.
Tote task status afterward indicates the command was successfully resent and the tote state updated.
healthy_conditions: Resend Tote Task is available on the tote management API page.
The correct tote can be selected or entered.
Get Tote Task shows updated tote status after the resend.
failure_conditions: The affected tote cannot be identified.
The Resend Tote Task function cannot be accessed or located.
The resend action does not clear the failed condition.
The tote continues to show an error or failed execution after the resend.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the resend action does not clear the failed condition.
Escalate if the tote continues to show an error or failed execution after the resend.
Escalate when deeper fault isolation is required because the source does not provide retry limits or additional troubleshooting steps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tote management API page showing Resend Tote Task and Get Tote Task.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: OCR: Resend Tote Task — Resends a command to a tote that encountered an error or failed to execute.

missing_details: The source does not provide the exact UI fields or button labels used to submit Resend Tote Task.
The source does not provide retry limits.
The source does not provide deeper fault isolation steps if resend fails.
The source does not provide explicit production stop or LOTO requirements.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this procedure.
